item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm beginning to suspect that making an SCP about Fifthism and Weezer may not be the great idea I once thought it was.

The more I ran with the idea the more I liked it, because Weezer is an inherently inexplicable band. Why did people hate Pinkerton so much? Why did Matt Sharp leave? Why does their style change so much? Why does Rivers still act like he's 22 when he's almost fifty? Why are there so many Weezer songs? I wanted to give those questions a weird, stupid answer.

Check out my brain child if you want, I'm afraid this is my peak:

www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13034663/scp-idea:weezer-the-redacted-album#post-4499500

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My intention for Fifthist Weezer was to never elaborate on how or why Weezer is Fifthist, because anything else would ruin the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am hellbent on making an SCP based on the fact that Weezer is Fifthist, even if it takes a million reviews to get the two green lights

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …So, did you happen to play the new Sekiro game yet? I think you might find something that's relevant to this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No Miss Saigon?

item.title: UPDATE FROM THE BIGGEST SELLOUT ON SCP
item.content:encoded: After this and other comments, object class has been updated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting, well-written and fun article, but I have to agree with the other posters that the Apollyon class doesn't feel necessary at all.

Still, the logs push me towards a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The anomaly is fun and very bizarre, and the log is well-written. Maybe a little too long for my taste, but I like it overall.

I'll have to agree with the statements regarding the Apollyon class. Throughout the whole article, I had a feeling that there is some aspect of this article that I don't understand.

Putting aside my thoughts about the "Apollyon" class as a whole, I can't see how this anomaly will destroy humanity and why is there nothing that can be done about it, which this class always implies.

UPD: After the Class change, I'm going to switch from novote to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so i upvoted and then noticed the apollyon and immediately slammed that downvote button. literally just the word "apollyon" was enough to change my upvote to a downvote. everyone is saying that apollyon is bad. you should probably change it.

aaaaaand that's an upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You lay out a series of real media works featuring the unusual spider phenomenon, then cleverly-crafted fictional stories to explore its effects, and ends with an unexpected spider-based incident, like the anomaly itself.

The Apollyon class is so…it's not necessary. The containment I'm also not sure of, as mentioned above, somehow modifying reality seems like a fix, but also there's the question of how exactly that was accomplished.

I'm going to novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My idea was that I was just going to reference something involving madama butterfly and spiders that already shows signs of being affected.

Bayonetta (2009 video game)

Synopsys: A gunslinging witch travels fights angels with the help of a demon named Madama Butterfly.

Summary of Changes: At the climax of the boss fight with Sapientia, rather than summon Hekatoncheir to perform the finishing blow, Bayonetta summons the demonic spider "Phantasmaraneae". Of note is that Phantasmaraneae is a canonical character, being a boss fight in Bayonetta 2.

By the way, in case you're wondering; that's exactly what happens in the real Bayonetta video game. I mean it already has Madama Butterfly and a giant spider eating someone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If other people have ideas for a collaborative test log, I'd be down, but if it's just one right now I'd like to keep the flow how it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Luckily for you, there is a giant spider demon that acts as a boss fight in the second game. Would you mind if I wrote an entry about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …i haven't played that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …what about the game Bayonetta, where Bayonetta's patron demon is literally named "Madama Butterfly"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't say that it doesn't affect normalcy, just that it has been altered. If I had to guess, the last log hints that the role between spider and human has been reverse, now with the spiders being the ones eaten by human characters.

item.title: w h e e z e
item.content:encoded:
I think I found the cover of the affected Weezer album.

Edit: After another read through I agree with several of the other comments as well. I like the article as a whole, but the Apollyon classification feels unnecessary. Other then that, I think it’s fine and dandy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs are so weird. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the class changed, I have no problem slamming that upvote button. The core motif is satisfyingly creepy, and the imagery in the logs is vivid and strong.

item.title: > a good use of apollyon
item.content:encoded:
wack

EDIT: With a Resurrection reference? <3

item.title: author fuck
item.content:encoded:
Why bother?
It's gonna hurt me!

Thanks Athos, Uncle Nicolini, and RockTeethMothEyes!

I forgot why I wrote this.

EDIT: 'Pataphysics is spelled with an apostrophe, so it's not a typo. Facts don't care about your feelings.
